title: End-to-End Release, Migration, Recovery, and TypeScript Engineering Defense · Inference Brief: Pokedex Ts Migration Rollback
summary: A polished candidate passes type checking and familiar tests but lacks emitted-runtime, abort, accessibility, fair-use, package, failure, migration, rollback, restore, and residual-risk proof. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts migration rollback increment in defensible recoverable TypeScript Pokedex release dossier. Learner…
objectives: Prove schema migration, old-package compatibility limits, backup, rollback, restored data identity, and support decisions.
Execute invalid input, not found, HTTP failure, malformed JSON, stale Promise, abort, cache corruption, write interruption, signal, and listener-leak cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Migration Rollback
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 1098 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend emitted and packed artifacts, migration, rollback, restore, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one command, token, EOF, abort, or output-width case and preserve the session model. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove schema migration, old-package compatibility limits, backup, rollback, restored data identity, and support decisions.
